description.value:
Nydraxis began as something outside the taxonomy of gods—an aberration born in a foreign plane where thought, matter, and memory were not distinct categories but interchangeable states of existence.

It was not worshipped. It was avoided.

Its nature was simple: to consume.

Not merely to destroy, but to incorporate. Every thing it devoured did not vanish—it was rewritten into its being. Stone, flesh, magic, time, identity: all became substrate for expansion.

Its existence changed irrevocably when it breached the Life Stream.

The Life Stream did not resist in a way mortal minds would expect. Instead, it was experienced as an overwhelming flood of structured existence—life, memory, divinity, possibility. Nydraxis did not merely consume it.

It drank.

And in doing so, it ascended.

What was once an aberration became something far more dangerous: a god whose divinity is built not on creation, but assimilation of creation itself.
